game_name: Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster's Hidden Treasure

danger_signals.visual_form: Control helmet worn by captured characters (Plucky Duck, Hamton J. Pig, Dizzy Devil, Calamity Coyote)
danger_signals.meaning: Friendly NPCs have been turned hostile and must be avoided or defeated; loss of safe allies
Major threat; defeating this entity will break control over allies
Obstacle foe blocking progression toward Montana Max

danger_signals.visual_form: Numerous hostile creatures populating bonus level
danger_signals.meaning: Contact = level failure; danger in resource-gathering context

safety_signals.meaning: Safe encounter; grants access to portal for level completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual elements reminiscent of Tiny Toon Adventures show logo
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage to Wackyland bonus level; optional resource opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Control device falls off and explodes after Gene Splicer defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Brainwashed allies are now safe; can be collected/rescued

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Playable character identity from Tiny Toon Adventures franchise
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in levels indicates safe zone progression path

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goal location; represents primary threat/conquest target
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Treasure location marks his territory; hidden across multiple levels

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Brainwashing helmet technology as faction signature
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mid-boss; controls multiple named allies as visual markers of control
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence indicated by brainwashed character appearances

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Turn hostile under helmet control; become safe allies when helmet destroyed
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Their presence in levels signals Gene Splicer's influence zone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Control helmet visible on Plucky Duck, Hamton J. Pig, Dizzy Devil, Calamity Coyote
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Controlled characters behave as enemies; must be avoided or defeated; attack Buster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Helmet explodes and falls off; character-specific comedic ragdoll physics activation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Freed allies become dazed or fall comically; transition from hostile to neutral/safe state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Montana Max remains as hidden objective; treasure trails across varied landscapes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple level progression required; lateral traversal through woods, snow, and other environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rainbow-colored portals (franchise logo callback) appear in levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional high-risk resource collection opportunity; Dodo contact = failure state

environmental_storytelling.example: Brainwashed allies scattered throughout levels visually communicate that Gene Splicer has infiltrated the world and compromised Buster's social network
Woods and snow environments signal different regions of journey toward Montana Max's hidden treasure; visual environment change marks quest advancement
Gogo Dodo's required discovery before portal access creates spatial storytelling—must explore to find safe passage forward
Wackyland as separate chaotic space contrasts with main narrative; populated densely with dangerous Dodos vs. controlled progression levels
Helmet explosion and character-specific comedic physics upon Gene Splicer defeat provides visual payoff to control-breaking narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Plucky Duck, Hamton J. Pig, Dizzy Devil, Calamity Coyote
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hostile engagement; must be defeated or avoided; attack Buster

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Plucky Duck, Hamton J. Pig, Dizzy Devil, Calamity Coyote
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Dazed or comical collapse state; no longer hostile; safe to approach
Boss combat encounter; controls multiple allies; must be defeated to progress narrative
Safe NPC interaction; grants portal access for level completion
Hazardous; contact causes level failure; create tension during resource collection phase
Standard foe obstacle; must be defeated to advance toward final objective
End-stage antagonist; must be traced down and defeated; triggers treasure rescue and Babs Bunny rescue narrative completion

notes: Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster's Hidden Treasure uses character state (brainwashed vs. freed) as primary world-state indicator. The control helmet serves as a visual language for corrupted allies. Environmental storytelling is driven by landscape variety (woods, snow) signaling quest progression. The game's danger and safety signals are tied to NPC behavioral states rather than abstract visual systems—friendly characters become threats when helmeted, and their visual transformation communicates world corruption. Gogo Dodo and portal systems create spatial storytelling through discovery mechanics. Wackyland bonus levels create narrative contrast through chaos and density. The paradigm is straightforward platformer progression with character-driven narrative manifestation rather than systemic world-state indicators.
sources_used: wikipedia - Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster's Hidden Treasure
